coaReleaseDateDownloadGuide.title: Conquest of Azeroth Release Date, Download, and Realm Access
coaReleaseDateDownloadGuide.subtitle: Conquest of Azeroth launches on the Vol'Jin realm on July 3, 2026. Use the official Project Ascension launcher, create one Ascension account, and select the Conquest of Azeroth realm from the launcher flow.
coaReleaseDateDownloadGuide.intro: The clean path from release time to character creation: launch date, official download, account setup, launcher choice, realm selection, and where to check live server status.

items.label: Full Release
items.time: 7:00 AM Pacific / 10:00 AM Eastern / 3:00 PM GMT
items.detail: The launch is set for the Vol'Jin Conquest of Azeroth realm, with the official release video announcing the same July 3 release window.

items.label: Launch Realm
items.detail: Vol'Jin is the Conquest of Azeroth launch realm shown on the official Ascension timeline.

items.label: Live Server Status
items.detail: The Ascension homepage shows server labels such as Login Server, Bronzebeard, Area 52, and Vol'Jin status so players can confirm the realm is online before logging in.

items.label: Official Download
items.detail: Download the official Ascension PC launcher. The launcher patches the game, manages approved addons, and lets players switch between Bronzebeard, Elune, and Conquest of Azeroth realms.

items.label: Account Setup
items.detail: Create one Ascension account with an email and password, then use that same account to log in through the launcher.

items.label: Open Beta History
items.detail: Earlier open beta events let players test all 21 classes on event realms, with level 45, level 55, and level 58 beta caps before the full launch path moved to Vol'Jin.

checklist: Create your Ascension account with email and password
Download the Project Ascension launcher and let it patch the game
Select the Conquest of Azeroth realm (Vol'Jin) from the realm list
Check the Ascension homepage to confirm the realm is online
Log in and create your first character from 21 original classes

coaBeginnerGuide.title: How to Start Playing Conquest of Azeroth
coaBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Start with the official launcher, choose the Conquest of Azeroth realm, create one of the 21 original classes, and follow your class trainer path early.
coaBeginnerGuide.intro: Conquest of Azeroth is not just a normal classless Ascension start. It uses 21 original Warcraft-inspired classes, custom talent trees, class trainers, new RPG creature stats, open-world item drops, and launch realm progression.

steps.title: Create an Ascension account
steps.description: Use the official Ascension sign-up flow with an email and password. The same account works through the Ascension launcher.

steps.title: Install the official launcher
steps.description: Download the Project Ascension launcher from the official download page and let it patch the game before choosing a realm.

steps.title: Select the Conquest of Azeroth realm
steps.description: For launch play, select the Conquest of Azeroth realm path (Vol'Jin) from the Ascension launcher and realm list.

steps.title: Choose one of 21 original classes
steps.description: Pick from classes such as Necromancer, Pyromancer, Cultist, Starcaller, Tinker, Reaper, Bloodmage, Guardian, Felsworn, Witch Hunter, Ranger, and more.

steps.title: Follow your early class quest
steps.description: Class trainers were added to essential starting zones, with an early prompt around level 3 to help players find their class quest and trainer.

steps.title: Choose your specialization at level 10
steps.description: Each class has three or more specializations, with a class tree and a specialization tree inspired by Dragonflight-style talent choices.

steps.title: Use talents to shape your role
steps.description: Many classes can cover more than one role, including tank, DPS, healer, or support. The Auto-Build option helps players avoid early talent confusion.

steps.title: Learn the rebuilt world systems
steps.description: Conquest of Azeroth adds RPG creature stats, immersive gear drops, open-world RPG items, new points of interest, custom areas, and expanded PvE and PvP options.

quickTips: One Ascension account works across all supported Ascension realms.
Use the Auto-Build option early to avoid talent confusion while learning your class.
Visit your class trainer in the starting zone to unlock your class quest early.
Open-world RPG loot means gear drops matter from the very first levels.

coaClassesGuide.title: All 21 Conquest of Azeroth Classes
coaClassesGuide.subtitle: Conquest of Azeroth features 21 original classes with three or more specializations, custom visuals, unique mechanics, and roles built around Warcraft lore.
coaClassesGuide.intro: Use this class grid to compare each class by role, armor, primary stats, specializations, core mechanic, and best-fit playstyle before choosing your launch character.

classes.name: Necromancer
classes.mechanic: Life Force fuels multiple undead pets and dark caster tools.
classes.bestFor: Players who want a pet-heavy dark caster with plague, frost, and undead swarm gameplay.

classes.name: Pyromancer
classes.mechanic: Fire magic, meteors, dragon-themed burst tools, and phoenix-style healing.
classes.bestFor: Players who want direct fire damage with an optional healing path.

classes.name: Cultist
classes.mechanic: Insanity rewards high damage but punishes uncontrolled buildup.
classes.bestFor: Players who like Old God fantasy, high-risk power, and dark hybrid roles.

classes.name: Starcaller
classes.mechanic: Lunar power shapes weapons, healing, barriers, and astral combat styles.
classes.bestFor: Players who want a flexible lunar knight with tank, healer, and ranged or melee options.

classes.name: Sun Cleric
classes.mechanic: Solar Power and Sunset stacks empower holy fire damage, defense, and healing.
classes.bestFor: Players who want holy fire fantasy with true hybrid role coverage.

classes.name: Tinker
classes.mechanic: Rounds, bombs, turrets, mechs, beacons, and nanobots control the battlefield.
classes.bestFor: Players who enjoy engineering tools, summons, gadgets, and utility-heavy gameplay.

classes.name: Runemaster
classes.mechanic: Attunement cycles through Frost, Fire, and Arcane while runes empower abilities.
classes.bestFor: Players who like technical caster gameplay, rune planning, and magic combos.

classes.name: Primalist
classes.mechanic: Earth, nature, wild gods, boons, animal spirits, and durable plate-based hybrid play.
classes.bestFor: Players who want a sturdy nature-themed hybrid with tanking, healing, and damage paths.

classes.name: Reaper
classes.mechanic: Soul harvesting, health spending, lifesteal, shadow tricks, and death-themed sustain.
classes.bestFor: Players who want a self-sustaining melee bruiser or shadow tank.

classes.name: Venomancer
classes.mechanic: Poison shapeshift forms, rot effects, healing-over-time support, stealth, and scorpion defenses.
classes.bestFor: Players who want poison, shapeshifting, and hybrid utility.

classes.name: Chronomancer
classes.mechanic: Order and chaos magic, wand ramping, time clones, rewinds, displacement, and healing utility.
classes.bestFor: Players who want a trickster mage with strong utility and healer options.

classes.name: Bloodmage
classes.mechanic: Blood magic spends health, builds Rage, siphons life, and uses transformation effects.
classes.bestFor: Players who want vampiric sustain, blood-fueled spellcasting, and flexible tank/healer/DPS paths.

classes.name: Guardian
classes.mechanic: Martial weapon mastery, shield tanking, banners, songs, and group support tools.
classes.bestFor: Players who want a clear plate fighter with safe tanking and party support.

classes.name: Stormbringer
classes.mechanic: Static stacks increase spell procs and damage while storm tools and air elemental support allies.
classes.bestFor: Players who want lightning caster gameplay with support pressure.

classes.name: Felsworn
classes.mechanic: Felfury Charges, Demon Form, and Felstrikes support agile melee, mobile caster, and dodge tank styles.
classes.bestFor: Players who want demonic mobility, burst combos, and fel-themed tank or DPS gameplay.

classes.name: Barbarian
classes.mechanic: Unbridled Rage builds in combat, with Enraged effects unlocking stronger attacks.
classes.bestFor: Players who want axes, bleeds, physical pressure, and party buffs.

classes.name: Witch Doctor
classes.mechanic: Potions, hexes, jinxes, healing wards, Loa magic, and dinosaur summons.
classes.bestFor: Players who want utility healing, damage-over-time pressure, and troll voodoo fantasy.

classes.name: Witch Hunter
classes.mechanic: Crossbows, muskets, silver shots, hounds, shadow tools, and dark tank sustain.
classes.bestFor: Players who want mobile monster-hunter gameplay with ranged damage or tank options.

classes.name: Knight of Xoroth
classes.mechanic: Deathfire builds like a finisher resource, powering hooks, chains, hellfire, demons, and tank tools.
classes.bestFor: Players who want a heavy hell-knight bruiser with apocalyptic melee and tank fantasy.

classes.name: Templar
classes.mechanic: Openers and Followups build into Combo Breaker finishing moves.
classes.bestFor: Players who enjoy combo timing, agile melee combat, and staff-based tanking.

classes.name: Ranger
classes.mechanic: Quiver abilities, poisons, melee ambushes, War Falcons, and support horns.
classes.bestFor: Players who want archer, outlaw, or wilderness support gameplay.

coaClassTierList.title: Conquest of Azeroth Class Tier List for Launch Starters
coaClassTierList.subtitle: A launch-focused recommendation grid for choosing your first class on Vol'Jin, built around official class roles, class mechanics, beta popularity notes, and beginner readability.
coaClassTierList.intro: This tier list is designed for the first character choice: smooth leveling, clear role identity, useful group tools, and forgiving mechanics matter more than chasing a damage chart before you have even installed the launcher.

tiers.label: Best First Picks
tiers.basis: Strong launch starter value, clear gameplay loop, role usefulness, and official beta popularity signals.

classes.name: Necromancer
classes.reason: One of the officially highlighted top popular classes, with a clear undead pet-summoner identity and a simple damage goal.

classes.name: Bloodmage
classes.reason: One of the officially highlighted top popular classes, with self-sustain, blood magic, and multiple role paths.

classes.name: Reaper
classes.reason: One of the officially highlighted top popular classes, with plate armor, lifesteal themes, and a self-sustaining melee style.

classes.name: Guardian
classes.reason: A direct plate fighter with tank, DPS, and support paths, making it one of the safest first choices for group play.

classes.name: Pyromancer
classes.reason: A readable fire caster with heavy single-target and AoE damage plus a Flameweaving healer option.

tiers.label: Flexible Strong Picks
tiers.basis: Great role coverage and strong fantasy, with slightly more class-specific systems to learn.

classes.name: Starcaller
classes.reason: Excellent role spread with lunar tanking, healing, and damage options.

classes.name: Sun Cleric
classes.reason: A full hybrid holy-fire class with Solar Power and clear spec identity.

classes.name: Primalist
classes.reason: Durable plate hybrid with earth, beast, and nature healing paths.

classes.name: Cultist
classes.reason: Powerful hybrid coverage with Old God mechanics, though Insanity management asks for more attention.

classes.name: Tinker
classes.reason: Great for gadget players who like turrets, bombs, rounds, beacons, and support tools.

classes.name: Chronomancer
classes.reason: Strong utility fantasy with rewinds, clones, displacement, wand builds, and healing tools.

classes.name: Felsworn
classes.reason: Mobile fel-themed melee, caster, and tank paths with Demon Form and Felfury Charges.

tiers.label: Good Specialist Picks
tiers.basis: Strong class fantasy and useful tools, best for players who already know which style they want.

classes.name: Venomancer
classes.reason: Poison shapeshifting is flexible and flavorful, with Rot, Vizier, Stalking, and Fortitude paths.

classes.name: Witch Doctor
classes.reason: Potion, hex, ward, and Loa gameplay offers strong utility for players who enjoy setup-heavy support.

classes.name: Witch Hunter
classes.reason: Mobile ranged and dark tank tools make it stylish, and its monster-hunter kit rewards active play.

classes.name: Ranger
classes.reason: Archery, Brigand, and Farstrider paths make it a strong pick for ranged damage and support horns.

classes.name: Knight of Xoroth
classes.reason: A heavy hellfire bruiser with Deathfire finishers, chains, demons, and tank options.

classes.name: Templar
classes.reason: Combo-based melee with Openers, Followups, and Combo Breakers, best for players who enjoy timing.

classes.name: Stormbringer
classes.reason: Static stacking, storm damage, and air elemental support create a strong caster-support identity.

classes.name: Barbarian
classes.reason: Axes, rage, bleeds, ale buffs, and ancestor tools work best for players who want physical pressure.

classes.name: Runemaster
classes.reason: Rune storage, attunement cycling, and spell combinations make it powerful for players who enjoy technical DPS.

coaTalentTreesAndBuildsGuide.title: Conquest of Azeroth Talent Trees and Builds Guide
coaTalentTreesAndBuildsGuide.subtitle: Learn how CoA builds work through Class Trees, Specialization Trees, level 10 spec choice, and optional Auto-Build support.
coaTalentTreesAndBuildsGuide.intro: Conquest of Azeroth uses 21 original classes, each with custom spells, talents, mechanics, and three or more specializations. At level 10, players choose a primary specialization, then spend points across both a Class Tree and a Specialization Tree to shape their role, combat style, utility, and class identity.

items.title: Talent System Basics
items.best_for: Players choosing their first specialization
core_data: Pick a primary specialization at level 10.
Use two trees: one Class Tree for class-wide tools and one Specialization Tree for the chosen spec.
Talent points alternate between Class Tree and Specialization Tree while leveling.
Auto-Build can automatically allocate talents based on the selected build.
items.player_tip: Choose Auto-Build when learning a class, then respec manually once the class loop feels natural.

items.title: Tinker Demolition Build
items.specialization_focus: Demolition focuses on explosives, Blackpowder, Explosive Rounds, Sapper Charges, Rocket Launchers, Handcannons, and Cannonballs.
items.best_for: Players who want a fast ranged DPS fantasy with explosives, gadgets, and battlefield tools.
items.player_tip: Use this as an early build card for players searching Tinker build, Demolition build, or explosive DPS.

items.title: Tinker Mechanics Build
items.specialization_focus: Mechanics specializes in turrets, land mines, intellect-scaling contraptions, and Shredder mechsuit gameplay.
items.best_for: Players who prefer setup-based combat, summoned devices, and mechanical control.
items.player_tip: Display this next to Demolition so visitors can compare direct explosions against turret planning.

items.title: Pyromancer Incineration Build
items.specialization_focus: Incineration burns enemies with direct fire damage, fire DoTs, meteors, molten rock, Ignite, and Blaze effects.
items.best_for: Players who want heavy single-target and AoE spell damage.
items.player_tip: Good for visitors searching best caster build, fire build, or beginner DPS spec.

items.title: Ranger Archery Build
items.specialization_focus: Archery Rangers fight from range, use Quiver abilities to modify damage, and rely on mobility to keep pressure on enemies.
items.best_for: Players who want a ranged physical build with movement and arrow-focused gameplay.
items.player_tip: Use this card for Ranger build, ranged DPS, and Archery spec searches.

items.title: Cultist Influence Build
items.specialization_focus: Influence Cultists act as dark healers, using Yogg-Saron-themed protection, absorb shields, healing, and Edict effects.
items.best_for: Players looking for a healer build with Old God flavor and support utility.
items.player_tip: Explain Insanity clearly because the class mechanic directly affects damage and risk management.

coaLevelingGuide.title: Conquest of Azeroth Leveling Guide
coaLevelingGuide.subtitle: Follow the main CoA leveling milestones from early class quests to level-cap testing, class trainers, talents, and endgame preparation.
coaLevelingGuide.intro: Leveling in Conquest of Azeroth is built around learning one of 21 original classes while exploring a Reborn Azeroth with RPG creature stats, immersive loot drops, open-world RPG items, class trainers, talent choices, and custom class gear. The official beta milestones moved through level 45, level 55, and level 58 event caps, while the alpha content also includes the level 60 endgame phase.

items.title: Start Your Class Journey
goals: Create a CoA character and begin learning the base class kit.
Watch for the level 3 class quest message.
Find your class trainer in starting zones such as Goldshire, Kharanos, or Brill.
items.player_tip: Do not ignore the trainer message; early pacing is built around finding the trainer and learning class quests.

items.title: Choose Your Primary Specialization
goals: Pick a primary specialization.
Open the talent interface and review both the Class Tree and Specialization Tree.
Use Auto-Build when you want the game to allocate talents while leveling.
items.player_tip: For a first character, choose the spec that matches your intended role before fine-tuning damage, utility, or PvP choices.

items.title: Build Your Core Rotation
goals: Spend talent points across both trees as they alternate while leveling.
Learn the class mechanic that defines your build, such as Tinker Rounds, Cultist Insanity, or Knight of Xoroth Deathfire.
Replace weak gear with items that support your class role and primary stats.
items.player_tip: Prioritize abilities that make your rotation stable before adding niche PvP or utility picks.

items.title: Prepare for Stronger Class Testing
goals: Refine class toolkit, interrupts, defensive buttons, and crowd-control usage.
Test polished class trainers and class quests.
Prepare for level 45 content such as the Zanzrakka world boss milestone from the March beta.
items.player_tip: This bracket is useful for learning what your class counters and how its control tools work.

items.title: Expand Spells, Gear, and Role Identity
goals: Visit class trainers to choose spells instead of relying on forced unlocks.
Test improved spell visuals, tooltip polish, and class-specific gear support.
Use the level 55 phase to compare class roles across all 21 classes.
items.player_tip: At this point, the best leveling build should balance clear speed, survivability, and simple resource management.

items.title: Final Open Beta Push
goals: Adventure through the June beta level 58 milestone.
Explore Reborn Azeroth features: RPG creature stats, immersive loot drops, and RPG items in the open world.
Polish your spec choice before stepping into max-level systems.
items.player_tip: Use this bracket to test whether your build works in open-world play, dungeons, and PvP pressure.

items.title: Move Into Endgame Systems
goals: Transition from leveling into endgame dungeons, raids, battlegrounds, arenas, world bosses, and the Manastorm.
Tune talents for PvE, PvP, or High Risk PvP instead of using one generic leveling setup.
Start collecting gear that supports your exact class role.
items.player_tip: Split leveling builds from endgame builds because dungeon, raid, arena, and High Risk PvP priorities are different.

coaDungeonsAndRaidsGuide.title: Conquest of Azeroth Dungeons and Raids Guide
coaDungeonsAndRaidsGuide.subtitle: Compare CoA dungeon and raid difficulties, from Normal dungeons to Mythic+ and Ascended raids.
coaDungeonsAndRaidsGuide.intro: Conquest of Azeroth brings Ascension-style PvE into a 21-class custom WoW framework. PvE content includes Vanilla dungeons across Normal, Heroic, Mythic, and Mythic+ modes, raids across Normal, Heroic, Mythic, and Ascended difficulties, original boss mechanics, expanded itemization, new world bosses, and class-tailored loot.

items.title: Normal Dungeons
items.content_focus: Classic Vanilla dungeon routes and baseline group play.
items.best_for: Leveling, first-time dungeon learning, and early gear upgrades.

items.title: Heroic Dungeons
items.content_focus: Powered-up versions of familiar dungeons with stronger tuning and better rewards.
items.best_for: Players ready to move beyond Normal dungeons without entering Mythic pressure.

items.title: Mythic Dungeons
items.content_focus: Harder dungeon difficulty with expanded dungeon mechanics.
items.best_for: Coordinated groups testing interrupts, defensive timing, crowd control, and class synergies.

items.title: Mythic+ Dungeons
items.content_focus: A full Mythic+ Vanilla Dungeon system for dedicated players who want to progress through 5-man content.
items.best_for: Groups that want repeatable endgame dungeon progression instead of only raid progression.

items.title: Flex Normal Raids
items.content_focus: Entry-level raid access for players who want to experience raid content.
items.best_for: Casual raid groups and first clears.

items.title: Flex Heroic Raids
items.content_focus: Harder raid tuning for groups seeking stronger rewards and cleaner execution.
items.best_for: Organized groups building toward Mythic and Ascended progression.

items.title: Mythic and Ascended Raids
items.content_focus: Expanded mechanics in Mythic and Ascended raids, with encounters designed to challenge CoA classes.
items.best_for: Guilds and high-skill groups chasing the hardest PvE content.

items.title: Molten Core
items.content_focus: Face Ragnaros and Molten Core bosses with 21 original classes and 69 specializations.
items.best_for: Players searching for Molten Core, Ragnaros, raid release, and legendary item content.

items.title: Onyxia
items.content_focus: Onyxia arrives on Rexxar as part of the level 60 alpha content path, with new treasure for CoA class gearing.
items.best_for: Players following the level 60 alpha raid path after Molten Core and other Vanilla raid milestones.

coaPvpAndHighRiskGuide.title: Conquest of Azeroth PvP and High Risk Guide
coaPvpAndHighRiskGuide.subtitle: Compare Battlegrounds, Arenas, World PvP, High Risk PvP, Crow's Cache, and Blood Bowl.
coaPvpAndHighRiskGuide.intro: CoA PvP is built around class identity, cleaner crowd-control roles, and Ascension's Hybrid Risk System. Players can choose PvE, PvP, or a separate High Risk PvP phase, then move into battlegrounds, arenas, world PvP events, and open-world risk-reward combat.

items.title: PvE Mode
core_rules: Players who choose PvE Mode are untargetable for open-world PvP unless they start PvP action first.
PvE and PvP players can still group, quest, trade, run dungeons, raids, and battlegrounds on the same realm structure.
items.best_for: Players who want CoA classes and PvE content without open-world ganking.

items.title: PvP Mode
core_rules: PvP Mode creates a full open-world PvP experience.
Players can seek out enemies in the open world while still sharing the broader world phase with PvE players for grouping and trading.
items.best_for: Players who want open-world danger without High Risk item loss.

items.title: High Risk PvP
core_rules: High Risk PvP is a separate phase.
Defeating a player in High Risk can make them drop a chest containing pieces of equipped and inventory gear.
High Risk players can find Bloodforged versions of dungeon and raid gear from open-world monsters.
items.best_for: Players who want the highest open-world stakes and accept losing gear in PvP combat.

items.title: Battlegrounds
core_rules: Level 60 Battlegrounds are part of the CoA endgame PvP feature set.
items.best_for: Players who want organized fights without open-world ambush rules.

items.title: Arenas
core_rules: CoA includes level 60 Arenas in 1v1, 2v2, and 3v3 formats.
The talent overhaul article also lists rated Arenas with 1v1, 2v2, and 3v3 solo queue as part of the level 60 endgame direction.
items.best_for: Players searching arena, best PvP class, duels, and small-team PvP builds.

items.title: World PvP Events
core_rules: World PvP Events include Crow's Cache and the Gurubashi Blood Bowl.
Crow's Cache centers on guild-vs-guild combat over a chest of spoils.
The Blood Bowl focuses on 1v1 gladiatorial combat and enemy-slaying streaks.
items.best_for: Players who want event-based PvP instead of random open-world fights.

items.title: Class Crowd-Control Identity
core_rules: CoA classes have fewer generic crowd-control tools than earlier versions.
Each class leans into more thematic control identity, such as Necromancer fear or Chronomancer slow.
Stealth uptime is restricted unless the chosen specialization focuses on stealth.
items.best_for: Players choosing PvP builds and trying to understand why one class controls fights differently from another.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Conquest of Azeroth

questions.question: What is Conquest of Azeroth?
questions.answer: Conquest of Azeroth is a Classic+ custom WoW MMORPG built on Project Ascension, featuring 21 original classes, 69 specializations, Dragonflight-style talent trees, immersive open-world RPG loot, dungeons, raids, and full PvP.

questions.question: When does Conquest of Azeroth release?
questions.answer: Conquest of Azeroth reaches full release on July 3, 2026 on the Vol'Jin realm, with the launch window announced in the official Ascension release video as 7:00 AM Pacific / 3:00 PM GMT.

questions.question: How do I download and play Conquest of Azeroth?
questions.answer: Download the official Project Ascension launcher from ascension.gg, create one Ascension account, let the launcher patch the game, then select the Conquest of Azeroth realm (Vol'Jin) from the realm list.

questions.question: How many classes are in Conquest of Azeroth?
questions.answer: Conquest of Azeroth features 21 original classes, each with three or more specializations, for 69 total specializations and 1000+ spells and talents across the class and specialization trees.

questions.question: Which realms support Conquest of Azeroth?
questions.answer: Conquest of Azeroth launches on the Vol'Jin realm. The Ascension launcher lets players switch between Bronzebeard, Elune, and Conquest of Azeroth realms using one account.

questions.question: Can I play Conquest of Azeroth with friends?
questions.answer: Yes. Conquest of Azeroth includes group PvE content such as dungeons and raids, plus Battlegrounds, Arena, and World PvP, so party play is a core part of the launch experience.

cta.title: Ready to Play Conquest of Azeroth?
cta.description: Download Ascension, pick from 21 original classes, and join the community on Discord and Reddit for builds, updates, and discussions.

access.description: Learn how to download, install, and access Conquest of Azeroth - the Ascension launcher, account creation, realm selection, and free-to-play setup.
access.metaTitle: How to Play Conquest of Azeroth - Download & Access Guide
access.metaDescription: Start playing Conquest of Azeroth with our download and access guide covering the Ascension launcher, account setup, realm selection, and free entry.
access.whatIsDescription: Access guides explain how to download the Ascension launcher, create an account, patch the client, and connect to the Conquest of Azeroth Vol'Jin realm - the custom 21-class WoW experience that is free to play.

whySteps.step1Description: Download and set up the Project Ascension launcher on Windows, with community Linux guides available
whySteps.step2Description: Register, verify, and prepare a free account for the Conquest of Azeroth realm
whySteps.step3Description: Select the Conquest of Azeroth realm and log in to start playing with 21 original classes

classes.title: Conquest of Azeroth Classes
classes.description: Explore all 21 original Conquest of Azeroth classes like Necromancer, Starcaller, Tinker, and Guardian with their roles, resources, and class fantasy.
classes.metaTitle: Conquest of Azeroth Classes - All 21 Original Classes & Specs
classes.metaDescription: Browse all 21 original Conquest of Azeroth classes and 69 specializations including Necromancer, Starcaller, Tinker, and Guardian with roles and playstyles.
classes.whatIsDescription: Conquest of Azeroth introduces 21 original classes and 69 specializations built on a Dragonflight-style system, each with unique resources, class fantasy, and role options like tank, healer, and DPS.

whySteps.step1Description: Match classes to tank, healer, melee DPS, or ranged DPS roles that fit your playstyle
whySteps.step2Description: Understand each class's unique resource mechanic, from Soul Shards to Arcane charges
whySteps.step3Description: Pick a class fantasy - undead Necromancer, celestial Starcaller, or inventive Tinker - that matches your theme

tier.title: Conquest of Azeroth Tier List
tier.description: Conquest of Azeroth tier list ranking all 21 classes and specs for PvE, PvP, DPS, healer, and tank roles, plus the best solo and endgame classes.
tier.metaTitle: Conquest of Azeroth Tier List - PvE & PvP Class Rankings
tier.metaDescription: See the full Conquest of Azeroth tier list with PvE and PvP class rankings, best DPS, healer, tank specs, and top solo classes for endgame.
tier.whatIsDescription: The Conquest of Azeroth tier list ranks the 21 original classes and 69 specializations by performance across PvE, PvP, leveling, and solo content, helping you choose the strongest option for your goals.

whySteps.step1Description: Identify top-tier DPS, healer, and tank specs for raids, dungeons, and arena
whySteps.step2Description: See which classes lead in PvE endgame versus High Risk PvP environments
whySteps.step3Description: Find the best solo class for open-world leveling and farming efficiency

talents.title: Conquest of Azeroth Talents
talents.description: Master Conquest of Azeroth talent trees, specializations, and class builds - Dragonflight-style trees, Auto-Build, and recommended builds for every class.
talents.metaTitle: Conquest of Azeroth Talents - Talent Trees & Class Builds
talents.metaDescription: Plan your Conquest of Azeroth talents with Dragonflight-style talent trees, specialization paths, and tested class builds for every role and spec.
talents.whatIsDescription: Conquest of Azeroth uses Dragonflight-style talent trees with alternating Class and Specialization points, giving every class flexible build paths and an Auto-Build option for new players.

whySteps.step1Description: Plan class and spec tree points before spending them in-game with the talent calculator
whySteps.step2Description: Let Auto-Build handle early points while you learn a new class's resources and rotation
whySteps.step3Description: Copy proven Necromancer, Valkyrie, and Cultist builds for PvE leveling and PvP

guide.title: Conquest of Azeroth Guide
guide.description: Complete Conquest of Azeroth guides for beginners - setup, first class pick, leveling route, talent basics, and open-world survival on the Vol'Jin realm.
guide.metaTitle: Conquest of Azeroth Guide - Beginner Setup & Leveling Walkthrough
guide.metaDescription: Start strong with our Conquest of Azeroth beginner guide covering setup, first class, leveling route, talents, and open-world tips for Ascension players.
guide.whatIsDescription: The Conquest of Azeroth beginner guide walks new players through launcher setup, account creation, first class selection, early leveling, talent basics, and open-world survival on the Vol'Jin realm.

whySteps.step1Description: Install the launcher, create an account, and log in to the Conquest of Azeroth realm
whySteps.step2Description: Choose a beginner-friendly class like Necromancer or Bloodmage for smooth leveling
whySteps.step3Description: Follow a proven 1-60 route using Auto-Build, class trainers, and open-world quests
